# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Kubernetes Pod specification for our container that needs to interact with the Docker socket for CI/CD operations. The pod needs elevated permissions to build and push Docker images. This runs in our build cluster for automated deployments.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: ci-cd-pod
spec:
  securityContext: {} # add here as necessary, e.g., seccompProfile and capabilities if needed for CI/CD operations with docker socket interaction   
  
# Mounts the Docker Socket to a volume in pod so that container can interact via UNIX domain sockets (TCP) from within Pod 
volumes:    
 - name: dockersock-volume # you need to make sure your host path is set correctly for this one and it has enough permissions. e.g., /var/run/docker.sock on all nodes of the cluster or an external volume with same access modes available in K8S pod spec file
   hostPath: 
     Path: "/var/run/docker.sock" # replace this according to your use case and permissions setting up for Docker Socket connection
   
# Container definition that uses image from registry (must have build permission) on docker socket, then builds a new one with the source code in /app directory as mounted volume at same path inside container. It also ensures 2 consecutive rebuilding triggers to ensure every time you do update/deploy Dockerfile or any content changes within your repo they get updated
containers:  
 - name: ci-cd     # assign a suitable and meaningful value, could be the image version etc as per requirement   
   image: registryImage  # set this with CI / CD tools like Jenkins for example. This has to include full buildx instruction in dockerfile e.g., .dockerignore & Dockerfile itself according how you setup it on your system/CI environment    
   command: ["/bin/sh"]    # Command is run inside the container by default, /sbin/init runs first as process 1 and rest starts with PID of 2 respectively depending upon K8s Node Affinity & tolerations. Replace '/app' for your codebase location in image
   volumeMounts:    
      - mountPath: "/app" # should match the repo dir on host where you are pushing images, if not set default is / app of container by docker/buildkit  e.g., ./application or similar according how repository setup under CI environment      
        name: dockersock-volume         # Name here must be same as volume defined above  
    readinessProbe:             # Optional; define when the pod can start running, and also stop if it fails to do so - useful for rolling updates. Set after container definition in .spec section e.,    
      exec:         
        command: ['docker', 'run', '-v', '/proc:/proc', ‘-v’, /sys:/sys',  …] # replace with your docker buildx/buildkit options based on the use case for image building via CI/CD operations. e.,
